name: S-37 (SS-142)
events: 9 Dec 1941 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) departed from Manila Bay for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Verde Island Passage.
19 Dec 1941 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) returns to Manila Bay to refuel and restore. She departed from for her 2nd war patrol the same day. Initially she was ordered to patrol the Calavite Passage.
2 Jan 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) patrols in the Basilan Strait.
4 Jan 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) patrols off Jolo Island.
12 Jan 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) patrols off Tarakan, Netherlands East Indies.
15 Jan 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) was ordered to leave the Tarakan area and proceed to Surabaya, Java, Netherlands East Indies.
23 Jan 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) ended her 2nd war patrol at Surabaya.
2 Feb 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) departed from Surabaya for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in Makassar Strait.
8 Feb 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) torpedoed and sank the Japanese destroyer Natsushio (offsite link) about 22 nautical miles south of Makassar, Celebes in position 05°10'S, 119°24'E.
18 Feb 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) patrols off Lombok Strait.
21 Feb 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.C. Dempsey) ended her 3rd war patrol at Surabaya.
27 Feb 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.R. Reynolds) departed from Surabaya for her 4th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Java Sea.
22 Jun 1942 With her overhaul completed USS S-37 (Lt. J.R. Reynolds) departed from Brisbane for her 5th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off the Bismarck Archipelago.

8 Jul 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.R. Reynolds) torpedoed and sank the Japanese transport Tenzan Maru (2775 GRT) about 30 nautical miles west-north-west of Rabaul in position 04°00'S, 151°50'E. S-37 is depth charged by the Japanese submarine chaser Ch-30 but she is able to escape undamaged.
21 Jul 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. J.R. Reynolds) ended her 5th war patrol at Brisbane.
17 Aug 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. T.S. Baskett) departed from Brisbane for her 6th war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off Guadalcanal.
13 Sep 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. T.S. Baskett) ended her 6th war patrol at Brisbane.
19 Oct 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. T.S. Baskett) departed from Brisbane for her 7th (and final) war patrol. She was ordered to proceed to Noumea to make a defensive patrol in that area.
5 Nov 1942 USS S-37 (Lt. T.S. Baskett) departed from her patrol area bound for Pearl Harbor. After an overhaul at San Diego she is assigned to training duties.
